title: Opening Weekend Box Office Chart Performance: Avatar Retains #1, Bone Temple Falls Short
text: James Cameron's Avatar: Fire and Ash maintained the top spot for its fifth consecutive weekend, earning $13.3 million over the 3-day period and $17.2 million for the 4-day MLK Jr. Day weekend. The film has accumulated $367.4 million domestically. Despite initial projections of $20-22 million, the horror sequel 28 Years Later: The Bone Temple underperformed significantly. The film earned only $13 million over 3 days and $15 million for the extended weekend, finishing in second place. Director Nia DaCosta's film represents the second-largest opening for the franchise, though it substantially underperformed the previous installment's $30 million debut. With a $63 million production budget exceeding the prior film's $60 million investment, profitability appears uncertain. The previous installment barely reached its break-even threshold of $150 million worldwide with $151.3 million in global revenue. However, the film received strong critical reception: a Certified Fresh 94% on the Tomatometer and a Verified Hot 91% on the Popcornmeter. The rest of the top 5 included Zootopia 2 at $12 million (4-day), The Housemaid at $10.1 million (4-day), and Marty Supreme at $6.7 million (4-day).
